name: Pork Cutlet with Orange and Thyme (Keebler)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound pork blade chops or pork tenderloin
1/2 cup fresh orange juice, or more
recipeInstructions:
1. Use a food mill or food processor to finely crumb the crackers.
2. Remove bones from the pork; cut and pound the pork into 3 to 4 inch cutlets, no thicker than 1/4 inch.
3. Press the pork into the crumbs to coat. Let the cutlets chill briefly in the refrigerator (this helps the crumbs stick without using egg or mayo).
4. Juice 1 small orange. Grate or zest the rind.
5. Heat the oil and margarine together in a skillet.
6. Fry the pork cutlets for about 1 1/2 minutes per side until golden brown.
7. Reduce heat to moderate and add the grated orange rind and thyme.
8. Add the juice (about 1/4 cup) and simmer for 4 to 5 minutes, allowing the sauce to reduce slightly.
9. Serve with Basmati rice (1/2 cup per person) and chopped broccoli side dishes, or zucchini and carrot julienne.
This recipe serves 2 to 3 people.
